releases.name: Architext 1.1.2
releases.summary: Patch release with dense spline routing and dense topology layout improvements.

releases.name: Architext 1.2.0 Release Truth
releases.summary: Release Truth makes release posture, scope, blockers, milestones, and historical trends first-class Architext data.

releases.name: Architext 1.3.0 Release Planning
releases.summary: Release Planning lets users craft one specific next release from cherry-picked roadmap items and ad hoc manually entered items without creating a second planning model.

releases.name: Architext 1.3.1 Serve Failure Correction
releases.summary: Patch release that replaced an uncontrolled serve-time failure with a controlled package-owned viewer error path.

releases.name: Architext 1.3.2 Schema Label Correction
releases.summary: Patch release that clarified package version versus data schema version and repaired stale manifest schema metadata through doctor/sync.

releases.name: Architext 1.3.3 Step Route and C4 Drilldown Corrections
releases.summary: Patch release with shared step-route pill rendering, C4 drilldown guidance and diagnostics, and drilldown-complete starter C4 data.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.0
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.0 shipped Rules, schema migration reporting, workflow views, PDF export, and Release Truth hardening.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.1 Verification and Rules Corrections
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.1 shipped Rules editor action placement, agent instruction rule migration, and the automated browser UAT harness.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.2 Sync Choice Reuse
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.2 shipped repository-level sync choice reuse and PDF self-data alignment.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.3 Serve Lifecycle Controls
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.3 adds explicit local serve lifecycle controls for foreground, background, browser launch, status, and stop workflows.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.4 Resilient UI Repair
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.4 keeps the served UI useful when target data is invalid and lets users run safe doctor/sync recovery actions from the browser.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.5 Serve Instance Management
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.5 makes background serve instances discoverable, addressable, restartable after CLI upgrades, and easier to update from npm.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.6 Ownership Language
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.6 clarifies target repository ownership in CLI help and README docs, and frames the checked-in Architext data as the real self-hosted architecture model.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.7 Release Truth Status Correction
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.7 prevents completed Release Truth items from appearing blocked in the UI and validates blocker/status exclusivity.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.8 Audit Hardening and Release Path UX
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.8 hardens local write boundaries, serializes dashboard write paths, improves release safety checks, and lets Release Path milestones collapse with compact completion counts.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.9 Release Path Milestone Status
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.9 keeps Release Path milestone status coherent with linked item completion so fully complete milestones no longer display as in progress.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.10 Viewer Interaction Corrections
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.10 makes foreground serve processes discoverable, keeps diagram legends above contained canvases, and enforces compatible Flow View and Flow selections.

releases.name: Architext 1.4.11 Diagram Instruction and Serve Port Corrections
releases.summary: Architext 1.4.11 strengthens diagram-maintenance instructions and restores automatic serve port fallback when the preferred loopback port is occupied.

releases.name: Architext 1.5.0 Diagram Semantics and Skill Release
releases.summary: Architext 1.5.0 ships stronger flow and sequence diagram semantics, printable Architext skill content, and completed audit-hardening work on the 1.5.0 data schema line.

releases.name: Architext 1.6.0 Routing Overhaul
releases.summary: Architext 1.6.0 rebuilds orthogonal edge routing around a single weighted-sum mount-cost objective, reduces and hops diagram crossings, coordinates reciprocal pairs, and derives mount spacing from the rendered arrowhead instead of magic numbers. A sanitized high-complexity corpus and a fitness ratchet lock the gains against regression. Also absorbs the audit-remediation and source/target-split work originally tracked as 1.5.1 (1.5.1 never shipped).

releases.name: Architext 1.6.1 Repository Intelligence and Diagram Config
releases.summary: Architext 1.6.1 carries the repository-intelligence and architecture-annotation work originally slated for 1.6.0, adds user-configurable diagram parameters (persisted in ~/.architext config), folds in the App-component decomposition and crowded-face routing refinements deferred from 1.6.0, and includes minor UI cleanup.

releases.name: Architext 1.6.2 Honest Routing Progress
releases.summary: Patch release that replaces the static route-planning spinner with honest live progress (phase labels, edge counts, routes-considered counter, elapsed clock) and repairs the roadmap release reference that broke deep validation.

releases.name: Architext 1.6.3 Server-Side Plan Precompute
releases.summary: Dense diagrams render instantly: architext serve precomputes every flow x view route plan in Node (where planning is ~20x faster than the browser) under strict input-key parity, and the viewer fetches finished plans with full in-browser fallback. Routing performance is now gated by a ratchet whose baseline only moves toward improvement, slow plans persist their per-phase timing breakdowns, winner selection is 1.27x faster with byte-identical routes, and the README/public face refresh is a release rule.

releases.name: Architext 1.7.0 Rust Rewrite (Zero Node)
releases.summary: Reimplement the entire product in Rust with NO Node dependency surviving — runtime AND build. One Rust routing core (native + WASM); validation, serve layer, and lifecycle ported; AND the React/Vite viewer rewritten as a Rust → WASM UI (Leptos/Dioxus/Yew, no JSX/Vite/npm) reusing the engine directly and implementing the Stitch design language. Native installers are the Node-free default; npm is an opt-in compatibility bridge only. Minor release: JSON data contract and CLI surface preserved exactly, proven by a byte-parity migration gate and a validation conformance corpus. Migration tooling (parity harness, ajv conformance, JS tests) is transitional and deleted with the JS code.

releases.name: Architext 1.7.1 Serve Startup Fix
releases.summary: Patch release: architext serve binds and opens its URL immediately instead of blocking on the full plan-farm precompute, so a large corpus no longer makes the CLI appear frozen during startup.

releases.name: Architext 1.7.2 Native Install + Self-Update
releases.summary: Native install + self-update release: a shell installer and an architext update/upgrade command download checksum-verified binaries from GitHub releases, so npm becomes an optional bridge users can leave. Releases now attach per-platform binaries plus SHA256SUMS, and an Architext release notifies the roboticus-site repository to re-sync.

releases.name: Architext 1.7.3 Diagram Fidelity + Distribution (routing-model groundwork)
releases.summary: Patch release. User-facing: sequence diagrams now follow the UML standard, the Sequence-mode flow-selection papercut and an overline-tracking drift are fixed, a Homebrew formula installs the native binary, and the serve plan farm warms incrementally so the first diagram appears fast. Under the hood, a from-scratch deterministic Rust routing model lands behind the ARCHITEXT_ROUTING_MODEL review flag (default routing is unchanged — still the engine): a locked shape law, a permanent forbidden-artifact gate, and crossings cut 28 -> 21, beating the engine on bend cost (beta 174 vs 1338) at equal crossings. That model is groundwork toward a future default switch, not a default change in 1.7.3.
